raw_consumer_statement:
I booked and prepaid an Executive Room in XXXX through Amex Travel on XX/XX/XXXX using my American Express Platinum Card. The booking confirmation and listing described a premium room category. 

When I checked in on XX/XX/XXXX, the hotel provided a materially inferior room category that did not match what was booked. I notified American Express immediately at check-in. American Express attempted to contact the hotel, but the hotel did not respond. I was provided a reference/chat ID ( ID XXXX ) and was instructed to submit a claim after checkout. 

I checked out on XX/XX/XXXX and submitted the claim ( dispute ID XXXX ) as instructed. American Express closed the claim immediately ( twice ), stating that the transaction was older than 60 days and therefore could not be investigated. I contacted American Express again to explain that this was a services-not-rendered issue and that the service date was XX/XX/XXXX, not XX/XX/XXXX. The claim was reopened and then closed again for the same reason without substantive review. 

This matter is not a billing error. It concerns failure to deliver the contracted travel service as described, where the relevant date is the date of service. It was not possible to know at the time of booking that the hotel would fail to provide the booked accommodation. I also satisfied all notice requirements by reporting the issue immediately upon arrival. 

I have supporting documentation, including booking confirmation, listing details, photographs showing the downgrade in room category, and ID record of my communications with American Express.
